heading: Unlawful access to stored communications
label: 2701 Unlawful access to stored communications
notes: Amendments2002—Subsec. (b)(1). , formerly § 225(j)(2)(A), as renumbered by , in introductory provisions, inserted “, or in furtherance of any criminal or tortious act in violation of the Constitution or laws of the United States or any State” after “commercial gain”.Pub. Short TitleThis chapter is popularly known as the “Stored Communications Act”.

children.text: intentionally accesses without authorization a facility through which an electronic communication service is provided; or
intentionally exceeds an authorization to access that facility;

children.label: A
children.text: a fine under this title or imprisonment for not more than 5 years, or both, in the case of a first offense under this subparagraph; and

children.label: B
children.text: a fine under this title or imprisonment for not more than 10 years, or both, for any subsequent offense under this subparagraph; and

children.label: A
children.text: a fine under this title or imprisonment for not more than 1 year or both, in the case of a first offense under this paragraph; and

children.label: B
children.text: a fine under this title or imprisonment for not more than 5 years, or both, in the case of an offense under this subparagraph that occurs after a conviction of another offense under this section.
by the person or entity providing a wire or electronic communications service;
by a user of that service with respect to a communication of or intended for that user; or
in section 2703, 2704 or 2518 of this title.
